name: Kings & Queens
description: Monarchs, royal lines, and coronations through history

questions.q: Queen Elizabeth II was married to which Prince from their wedding in 1947 until his death in 2021?

source.notes: Philip, Duke of Edinburgh, was her consort for 73 years.

questions.q: Which British monarch reigned during the 1969 moon landing?

source.notes: Reigned 1952–2022.

questions.q: British monarchs are traditionally crowned at which church?

source.notes: Every coronation since 1066 has been held at Westminster Abbey.

questions.q: Prince Albert was the devoted husband of which long-reigning British queen?

source.notes: Married 1840; Albert died 1861, Victoria wore black for the rest of her life.

questions.q: Which English king is famous for having six wives?

source.notes: Reigned 1509–1547: Catherine of Aragon, Anne Boleyn, Jane Seymour, Anne of Cleves, Catherine Howard, Catherine Parr.

questions.q: Which King of France was known as the 'Sun King' and built the Palace of Versailles?

source.notes: Reigned 72 years (1643–1715); longest verified reign in European history.

questions.q: What was the name of Henry VIII's first wife, who he divorced to marry Anne Boleyn?

source.notes: Married to Henry for 24 years — his longest marriage.

questions.q: Henry VIII had how many wives in total?

source.notes: Rhyme: 'Divorced, beheaded, died, divorced, beheaded, survived.'

questions.q: Richard III, the last English monarch to die in battle, fell at which 1485 battle?

source.notes: Defeated by Henry Tudor (later Henry VII), ending the Wars of the Roses.

questions.q: Queen Elizabeth I ruled England for approximately how many years?

source.notes: Reigned 1558–1603.

questions.q: Which French king was Marie Antoinette's husband, executed by guillotine during the French Revolution?

source.notes: Executed January 1793; Marie Antoinette followed him in October.

questions.q: Which English queen, a great-granddaughter of Henry VII, was declared monarch for just nine days in 1553 before being deposed and later beheaded?

source.notes: Reigned 10–19 July 1553. Executed February 1554.

questions.q: Which English king was nicknamed 'The Lionheart'?

source.notes: Reigned 1189–1199; spent most of his reign on Crusade.

questions.q: What was the name of William the Conqueror's wife?

source.notes: Matilda of Flanders; mother of William II and Henry I.

questions.q: Which British king was on the throne when the American colonies declared independence in 1776?

source.notes: Reigned 1760–1820.

questions.q: Who was the first Tudor King of England?

source.notes: Won the crown at Bosworth Field in 1485; devised the Tudor Rose.

questions.q: How old was Queen Victoria when she acceded to the throne in 1837?

source.notes: Became queen within weeks of her 18th birthday.

questions.q: Which Holy Land queen-consort married both Louis VII of France and later Henry II of England?

source.notes: One of the most powerful women of the Middle Ages; mother of Richard the Lionheart and King John.

questions.q: Which English king founded Eton College in 1440?

source.notes: Founded as 'The King's College of Our Lady of Eton beside Windsor'.

questions.q: The 1605 Gunpowder Plot aimed to assassinate which king?

source.notes: Foiled November 5, 1605 when Guy Fawkes was arrested.

questions.q: Which English king founded the Order of the Garter in 1348?

source.notes: Britain's oldest surviving order of chivalry.

questions.q: Who was the last monarch of the House of Stuart, reigning from 1702 to 1714?

source.notes: Anne oversaw the 1707 Acts of Union that united England and Scotland.
